title: microchilus procerus
intro: Microchilus procerus, also known as the "yellow mountain orchid," is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Microchilus procerus are said to include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and even exhibiting antiviral and antifungal properties. Microchilus procerus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine practices in Southeast Asia.
